insights: Location-category interaction effects explain nearly twice as much variance in resolution time (1.9%) as location (1.0%) and category (0.8%) main effects combined, indicating that budget and workflow efficiency improvements must target specific geographic-category pairings—not locations or categories in isolation.

The three worst-performing location-category combinations—United States-Database (158.38 days), Canada-Inquiry/Help (149.27 days), and Canada-Network (146.61 days)—exhibit large unexplained resolution time gaps (+37.03, +22.68, and +22.43 days respectively) beyond temporal concentration, signaling high-priority operational bottlenecks requiring root-cause investigation.

The three best-performing combinations—India-Network (96.14 days), India-Hardware (96.06 days), and Canada-Software (98.41 days)—show strong location-category synergy, with India-based pairs benefiting from both interaction effects and a systemic location advantage (India has the shortest overall mean resolution time: 108.15 days).

Neither priority distribution (P1+P2 concentration correlates weakly at r=0.373) nor assignment group allocation (100% category-aligned, thus redundant) explains the observed interaction effects—ruling out common workflow governance levers and pointing to deeper process, tooling, or cross-functional coordination issues within specific location-category contexts.

Temporal concentration—i.e., when tickets are opened—explains only 11.9% of the variation across the six key location-category combinations, confirming that calendar-driven seasonality is not the primary driver of resolution time disparities; instead, structural or procedural factors unique to each pairing dominate.

The systematic degradation of timestamp integrity over time (negative duration rate rising from 13–48% in early 2023 to 57–90% in late 2023) reflects an underlying ETL or data entry process failure that likely coincides with increased volume or staffing changes—posing a latent risk to future expense tracking reliability if unaddressed.
summary: To improve budget allocation and workflow efficiency, the analysis reveals that resolution time variation is overwhelmingly driven by location-category interactions—not standalone geography or expense type. The United States-Database, Canada-Inquiry/Help, and Canada-Network pairings represent critical operational bottlenecks with unexplained delays exceeding 22 days, while India-Network and India-Hardware demonstrate replicable efficiency advantages rooted in both interaction synergy and broader location-level performance. Conventional levers like priority routing and assignment group alignment have been ruled out as explanatory factors, and seasonal timing accounts for less than 12% of the variation—indicating that process design, tooling consistency, or cross-regional handoff protocols are the likely root causes. Given the confirmed deterioration in timestamp integrity over 2023, immediate attention should be directed to stabilizing the incident logging pipeline before expanding analysis to cost or approval-time dimensions.
